title: Chapter 34
text: Chapter Thirty - One L ara squeezed the trigger. The pistol fired, and her arms jerked, but she kept her hold and leveled the barrel quickly. The gunshot echoed through the chamber, and her ears rang even with the earplugs Ronin had given her . He tipped his head forward, chin brushing her shoulder. His solid presence at her back was a big help in controlling the gun, but it was as much a matter of knowing what to expect. She’d learned a lot since she shot at him back in Cheyenne . Not that her aim had improved much . “One foot, two inches to the left and ten inches down from your mark.” Ronin’s voice was clear through the earplugs, thanks to the tiny electronics he said were inside them. That he could figure the distance so accurately was as amazing as it was discouraging . She glared at him over her shoulder . “It’s a little closer than before,” he added, smirking . “By what? An inch ?” “Five-eighths of an inch, actually .” Lara rolled her eyes and faced forward. Her target was a small circle drawn on a wooden plank, twenty feet away. She couldn’t tell any of her shots from the other holes, but she trusted Ronin . Taking a deep breath, she aimed the pistol and pulled the trigger. It boomed again. Wood splintered at the top left corner of the board . “ Closer . Only — ” “What is the point?” Lara asked, flicking on the safety and lowering her arms. “What kind of damage can this do ?” “None, if you don’t start improving your aim .” She turned toward him, narrowing her eyes. “ Ronin .” “It depends what you’re shooting at .” “You know damn well I’d be shooting at a bot .” “Not all bots are built like me.” He banged his knuckles on his chest. The solid thud was muted by his clothes, but she knew well what was under his skin. “That pistol would barely put a scratch in my casing. But not all of Warlord’s gearheads are outfitted for war .” “How much use am I going to be? I’m not a soldier, Ronin. Shouldn’t I leave the shooting to them ?” “ You are the one who insists on going. You know what use you will be, and if it goes as planned, you won’t have to fire a single shot. But I won’t have you there, away from me, without some way to defend yourself .” “There will be soldiers with me .” Gripping her shoulders, Ronin turned her back toward the target. “None of them are me.” He slid his hand slowly along her arm before settling it over hers . Lara’s skin tingled with his touch. When he moved closer, pressing his body to her back, her breath quickened . In the two months since they came to the base, their physical contact had been gentle, warm, and affectionate, but lacked the intimacy she’d come to crave. She knew he was worried about hurting her, and hated that he thought she was so fragile, even after the splint had come off a few days ago . “Both eyes open.” His voice was so close to her ear that she could almost feel his non-existent breath against her skin. “Point your whole arm directly at the target, in one straight line. Imagine that line continuing from the barrel, and line it up with where you want to hit. Then squeeze the trigger .” There was something else she’d rather imagine . She took aim along the barrel and clicked off the safety. Ronin withdrew his hand. Lara squeezed the trigger three times, pausing between each only long enough to adjust for the kick . “Six inches low, two to the — ” “I’m done,” she said, plucking out the earplugs and dropping them into the pocket sewn on her skirt. Turning to him, she wrapped her arm around the back of his neck, holding the pistol loosely, and pressed her body against his . Ronin leaned forward and raised an arm, reaching behind his head to pluck the pistol from her grip. He engaged the safety and returned the gun to the holster on his thigh. “You’re never going to learn anything new if you allow yourself to get so frustrated .” “I’m not frustrated.” She brushed her mouth over his and nipped at his lower lip. “I’m distracted .” Ronin released a groan. He wrapped an arm around her, slipping his fingers into her hair. “What is distracting you, Lara Brooks ?” Lara trailed kisses over his jaw and down his neck. “Your hands, on my skin.” She pressed her pelvis against him, and didn’t miss the bulge against her stomach. She was bare beneath her skirt; he could be inside her with no effort. “Your body, and how I want to run my hands all over it .” Skimming a hand down his torso, she wrapped her fingers around his cock. “I miss you inside me,” she said into his ear . He shuddered. She took pleasure in him being as affected as she by their lack of physical intimacy . “You’re still healing,” he said, voice barely more than a whisper. He brushed his cheek over her hair and dropped his hands to her hips, forcing their bodies apart . She clasped his arms before he pulled away; she wouldn’t let him use that excuse any longer. “I’m not made of glass, Ronin .” He slid his hands, slowly, toward her rear. “ I know …” “Then why haven’t you touched me ?” “I have,” he replied. “ I am .” Lara stepped closer. His fingers flexed on her ass, and an ache blossomed between her legs. She wanted those hands everywhere . His eyes focused on her chest as she unbuttoned her shirt. “Touch me some more .” “ Lara …” She cupped his jaw and his eyes met hers. His pupils expanded, his entire focus solely on her. “I want you to fuck me, Ronin. Love me. Like before.” She stood on her toes and leaned forward, her lips a hair’s breadth away from his. “You won’t hurt me . I — ” Lara’s heart sped with his sudden movement. The distance between their bodies vanished as he claimed her mouth with his and grasped the backs of her thighs, lifting her. She wrapped her legs around his hips, her skirt bunching high. Cold concrete pressed to her back . She kissed him in desperation, driven by a burning need stronger than she’d ever experienced. Too long. She’d gone too long without this. He ground his pelvis against her exposed sex, and she gasped, pleasure shooting through her limbs . Ronin flattened a palm against the wall and deftly unfastened her remaining buttons, exposing her breasts to the cool air. He cupped one, stroking her nipple between forefinger and thumb, and Lara moaned against his lips . Keeping an arm around his neck, she slid the other between their bodies and opened his pants. His cock, hard and ready, sprang free. She grasped it, guiding the head to her center . “Now, Ronin,” she whispered . He didn’t hesitate . Lara exhaled, tilting her head back as Ronin thrust in and filled her. She tightened her legs around him, drawing him closer, deeper. His hands fell to her hips, bracing her before he withdrew and slammed back into her eager body . Reality melted away, leaving only sensation; his earthy scent, the feel of his warm, solid body, the pressure of his fingers on her skin, the delightful friction between them . He was all she needed to feel alive . She closed her eyes and clung to him, panting in time with his strokes. Her climax struck like a bolt of lightning, quick and forceful, and her cries echoed off the concrete walls. Pleasure swept through her, lifting her higher and higher. Her fingers dug into his shoulders and she bit her lip to muffle her screams . “Lara,” he groaned, and his rhythm, so perfect, faltered. For an instant, she thought she heard the machinery inside him whirring. His cock vibrated, drawing another orgasm from her. Then he sagged forward, burying his face between her neck and shoulder, and braced one hand against the wall for support . Panting, Lara smiled and brushed her fingertips over the short hairs on the back of his head. His cock still filled her, and she was tempted to have him take her again . “I missed this,” she said, opening her eyes, and kissed his temple . “One of these times, my system isn’t going to recover .” Lara laughed . Ronin lifted his head and looked at her, smirking. “Is this all I have to do to hear that sound from you ?” Her smiled faded. She caressed his cheek. “We haven’t had much to laugh about lately .” “Did we ever? We’re both alive. That’s something to celebrate, isn’t it ?” “Yes.” She pressed her lips to his . They were both alive now, but that did nothing to diminish her fears about going back into Cheyenne. It was the right choice, and the most dangerous one. It would’ve been so easy to stay in the base, to do their share of work, to live their lives in peace . But how would she sleep at night, knowing the people of Cheyenne suffered while she lived in security and comfort ? Ronin was her happiness. With him by her side, there was hope . She traced his brow with her fingertip and looked into his eyes. “I love you .” “I love you, too.” He settled his palm over her cheek, thumb brushing over her tiny scar. There was impossible strength in his hand, but she knew he’d never misuse it . She smiled and tightened her legs, unwilling to relinquish him. Moments like this were few, and though her need was satisfied, she craved more . The scrape of the metal door was the only warning that their privacy had come to an end . * * * R onin detected the sound three tenths of a second before Lara tensed . “Shit,” she muttered, pulling her shirt closed . He quickly set her on her feet, smoothed down her skirt, and steadied her when she staggered. Rapid boot steps approached . Someone cleared their throat behind Ronin. “ I … uh …” Lara glanced over Ronin’s shoulder, grinning, and turned away to button her shirt . Ronin tucked his penis into his pants, refastened them, and faced the newcomer. His processors cycled through countless potential reactions to the interruption, most of them laced with some sort of annoyance. They’d been down here, alone, for the better part of an hour without any disturbances, but once they gave into their desires … He recognized the soldier, though he didn’t know his name. The young man’s face was redder than Lara’s. Ronin raised his brows. It was the most neutral response he could muster. He yearned for Lara’s heat; two months of simulations and memories had only heightened his desires . She placed her hand on his back. He wanted nothing more than to turn around, press her against the wall, and have her again . “C-Colonel w-wants you to report to the war room,” the soldier stammered. “Please?” His eyes flicked past Ronin, to Lara. He knew what the soldier would see — flushed cheeks, swollen lips, and disheveled hair. A woman well- loved . Logic — the soldier was young and inexperienced, only following orders. He had unwittingly walked into a private moment and was uncomfortable because of it. Unsure of how to act . But a part of Ronin — thankfully small — wanted to drag the soldier out of Lara’s view and ensure he never looked upon her again . “We’ll be there soon,” Lara said . Ronin was grateful for her levelheaded response; he couldn’t have offered the same . His jealousy and possessiveness were entirely irrational, but he could no more deny them than the data in his memory . Ronin stared at the soldier. The young man swallowed and looked at the floor . “We can find our way,” Ronin finally said . “Y-yes sir.” Cheeks flaring red, the soldier turned and hurried away. The slamming door echoed behind him . “Scaring boys, now?” Lara asked with a laugh . Ronin faced her, pausing to appreciate the pink on her cheeks, her tousled hair, her enticing lips. They were for him . “I didn’t do anything .” “You didn’t have to.” She took his hand, lacing their fingers together. “Let’s go see what He-Who-Must-Be-Obeyed wants. The longer he waits, the more pissed he’ll get .” They walked down concrete corridors, through heavy steel doors, beneath tired overhead lights. The base’s security was undeniable — anyone who hadn’t spent a considerable amount of time within would get lost quickly. Even Ronin was close to losing track of the number of defensible chokepoints and labyrinthine passages . Yet something in Ronin still longed for the outside, for the Dust. For open air and relative quiet. Lara mentioned several times that she felt cooped up, like a prisoner, and he understood. Freedom to roam most of the facility freely couldn’t replace the feel of wind and sunlight, even for a bot . The interrogation room — now informally referred to as the war room — was full of soldiers when Ronin and Lara arrived. As usual, Rodriguez sat at the head of the long table, now with Captain Cooper to his right and the synth sergeant, Maul, to his left. William Anderson was also present, seated beside Newton . “Close the door,” Rodriguez said, and one of the soldiers obeyed. “We’re going to go through all of this quickly. We’ve all been briefed, and we’ve all sat through thirty of these meetings .” He met Lara’s gaze as she and Ronin sat down. “And no damned interruptions today, Brooks. This is all finalized now. Is that clear ?” Ronin dipped his head, hiding his involuntary smirk. Lara and the Colonel had gotten into at least one argument during every meeting. This marked the twenty-seventh such gathering; Rodriguez was close in his count, but not entirely accurate . Lara raised her hands in mock surrender . “ Fine .” Rodriguez regarded her for several seconds, eyes narrowed. “All right, people. You have your team assignments, and you have your objectives. We’ll have an FOB established to the west of town, where we can evacuate the injured to be taken care of by Nancy’s people. Alpha team will head over the wall and secure the hospital, from which they’ll push into the bot district. That should create enough trouble to call in Warlord’s bots from outside the area . “While they’re distracted, Lara will attempt to rally human support. Bravo team, you’re on her like flies on shit. If she’s successful, Bravo will escort her and Newton into the Market, and he will attempt to sway the bots to our cause .” “The human settlement is usually clear,” Lara said. “Gearheads only go there randomly, looking for rulebreakers to punish or desperate people to toy with. Otherwise, we could suck dirt for all the shits they give. The hard part is going to be getting into the Market without raising suspicion .” Rodriguez pressed his palms to the tabletop, fingers splayed, and took a slow, deep breath. “There are a lot of moving parts here, and we’re relying on some pretty big variables. We have no guarantee that any of these people, flesh or metal, will support us. I don’t have to remind any of you that this is the most significant operation any of us has ever attempted. This isn’t a scrap run into the wasteland. This is a war . Our goal is to win it without anyone getting hurt, but best-case isn’t what you plan for in these situations .” Rodriguez tilted his head towards Captain Cooper, who leaned forward and took over. “We’ve scouted this place thoroughly over the last few weeks. Security’s lax, but we’ve identified at least twenty-five unique tangos, equipped with various small arms. They are all confirmed as bots. We don’t know if any of them have reinforced casings, so we must assume that our weaponry will only be effective within about fifty yards . “We’re not going to be able to use radios. Most bots have internal antennas, Anderson says because they used to connect to wireless networks, or something. Anyway, that means that they’ll be able to detect our communications…so we’re going to have to listen for gunfire as our signal . “Stick to your squads, watch each other’s backs, and be mindful of cover. We don’t want to lose anyone, but it’s going to be real bullets when they return fire, and none of us are bulletproof .” It only took one shot in the right spot to take down a bot. Reinforced casings couldn’t handle the armor-piercing ammunition that was deployed in the latter days of the War, according to Newton. Ronin had dealt with enough holes to know the truth of it . “And what about Warlord?” Lara asked . “We’ve had a positive ID, but he doesn’t seem to stick to a particular habit. Goes to Kitty’s a few nights a week, never at the same time. We’re hoping all this will flush him out,” Cooper replied . “He’ll come,” she said. “Bastard likes to take care of things himself, usually. Problem is, will he go hide once he realizes what’s happening ?” “I believe we can provide a little insight,” William said, clearing his throat. “I don’t know how much use it will be , but …” “We have to treat a portion of this mission as a manhunt.” Rodriguez’s expression was stoic, his gaze shielded. “Warlord must be captured or deactivated. Any intel will be useful .” William nodded, gesturing to Newton. The bot leaned forward, metallic fingers gliding over a small console inlaid in the table. The lights in the room dimmed, and a screen came on behind Rodriguez, slowly gaining brightness . “Newton and I have been, with Nancy’s help, delving into the old records on file here. A good portion of the data is corrupted and inaccessible, and most of what we can access is irrelevant, but we’ve found some information on Warlord that may be pertinent to understanding his state of mind .” A grainy, pixelated image appeared on the screen. Rodriguez slid his chair aside . On the screen, a man sat at a table in an office. The image clarified as the recording played . Lara grasped Ronin’s hand, squeezing . The man was Warlord . He was bald, cheeks gaunt, and dark circles cradled his sunken eyes, but it was unmistakably Cheyenne’s tyrannical ruler . “You understand the risks involved, Mr. Turner?” a female, off-camera, asked. Her voice crackled over the unseen speakers in the war room . Kevin Turner, the man who would become Warlord, laughed bitterly. “Yeah. But, look at me, Doctor Yuan. Death isn’t a risk, it’s an inevitability. If this means a little more time with my family…” He dropped his gaze and ran his tongue over his lips . “In all honesty, Mr. Turner — ” “Kevin, please. I don’t really have it in me to deal with formalities anymore, you know ?” “Kevin. In all honesty, the chances of this procedure being successful are very small. I have to warn you that you might prematurely end what time you have remaining with them .” He shook his head, sliding a palm over the surface of the table absently. “Maybe. But what more time do I have with them right now? A few weeks for them to watch me die? At least this way, it’s on my terms. And they don’t have to …” Turning away from the camera, Kevin lifted a trembling hand to his face, covering his eyes as tears welled. “They don’t have to see me suffering. Don’t have to watch me become a shell of the man I was, and…and my family won’t have to feel so damned helpless while it all happens .” When he dropped his hand, moisture glistened in his eyes, and there were splotches of color on his cheeks. He faced the camera directly, and though it was a recording from two-hundred years before, the despair, desperation, and agony in the man’s expression was clear . “I want more time with Diane. I want to see my kids grow up .” The video flickered, cutting to a high-angle view of an operating room. Several individuals in green scrubs, wearing facemasks and gloves, worked around a table. A motionless, human-shaped form lay atop it, draped in a blanket. One of the surgeons stepped aside, allowing the camera view of the subject’s exposed brain . “The connections for the implant are complete,” said Doctor Yuan. One of the others wheeled a steel cart to her, and she lifted an electronic device from atop it. “Once this is inserted, we can begin the upload .” The war room was silent as the recording’s speed increased, moving the surgeons ten times faster. When it slowed to normal, there were alarms beeping in the room, and the surgeons’ voices were frantic . “He’s flatlining .” “We need the paddles — ” “No!” Doctor Yuan shouted. “They’ll fry the implant !” “We can’t just let him die, Jessica !” “He was already dead. The research is what matters now, and it’s the only chance he has of seeing his family again .” “That’s not how it’s supposed to be, Goddammit !” “If we succeed, it won’t ever have to be like this again .” “We can at least perform CPR — ” Doctor Yuan shook her head. “The implant is too delicate. We can’t risk disrupting the connection, or we might do irreversible damage to his consciousness .” “How much damage have we done, already ?” “He accepted the risks. You did, too, when you joined this project .” The continuous tone of the heart monitor punctuated her statement for six long seconds before the recording flickered again . Kevin Turner sat at a table in a nondescript room. A dozen wooden blocks painted different colors lay scattered before him. His cheeks were full, there was a healthy color to his skin, and dark hair atop his head . “I don’t—” The voice was Kevin’s, but his mouth didn’t move until he repeated the words. “I don’t want to do this anymore .” “A little at a time, Kevin,” Doctor Yuan said from off-camera. “It’s perfectly natural that things are difficult for you now. Though the ultimate functions and appearance are the same, your mind is going to take time to learn how to control your body. Please, try once more .” Frowning, Kevin raised his arm above the table. The movements were wobbly, jerky, imprecise. His brow furrowed as he reached forward, guiding his shaky hand toward one of the red blocks. Tentatively, he pinched the block between forefinger and thumb, and lifted it off the table . “Good. Would you please stack it atop one of the same color?” the Doctor asked . His eyes flicked down to the other blocks, pupils dilating and shrinking. Slowly, he moved his hand, hovering it over first a green block, and then a blue . “It’s okay if you are uncertain about the colors, Kevin. It’s not your fault,” a male said from off-camera. “We just need to know, so we can make the necessary adjustments to get you back to normal .” “Doctor Anderson is right, Kevin,” Doctor Yuan agreed. “We’ll take things one step at a time .” With a slight, unnatural nod, Kevin lowered his hand. Just before the block touched the one beneath it, his arm jerked. The blocks slammed together. The red block flew from his grasp, clattering on the floor . “Dammit!” Kevin roared through his closed mouth. He swung his arm over the surface of the table, clumsily sweeping all the blocks off. “I told you I don’t want to do this !” “It’s all right, Kevin,” Anderson said. “We can be done for today .” Another flicker, and the scene changed. Kevin paced in a carpeted room. A beige couch and two matching chairs rested nearby. A woman — blonde, somewhere in her forties, perhaps — sat in one of the chairs, wringing her hands in her lap . “Kevin, please,” she said . “Please, Diane? I don’t know what you expect. You tell me that and I’m supposed to be cool and collected after all this time ?” “Just…be patient, please. We knew this would take time, and it’s worked out better than we hoped. You’re still here , Kevin.” She pressed her hands down on her thighs and bowed her head. “We want you to come home, but the psychiatrists say you’re still…adjusting to the changes .” “They won’t let me see the kids, Diane. My fucking kids! It’s been eight months, and I haven’t seen them since I first woke up. I don’t sleep, I don’t eat, I don’t use the fucking bathroom. All I do is think about the life I’m supposed to be living .” Diane stood and approached him. She reached out and hesitantly placed a hand on his arm. Kevin jerked away from her, turning and taking several steps back. Diane’s hand fell, and her face crumpled . “I know it’s hard — ” “You know?” he demanded, whirling on her. She retreated from his advance, stumbling around one of the chairs. He grabbed it and flung it against the wall, shattering the frame. “You think you have any fucking idea what this is like ?” Diane held her hands out. “Kevin, stop! Don’t do this !” “You, too?” He dug his fingers in his neck and pulled, tearing off a chunk of synthetic skin to reveal the metal plates of his jaws and cheek. “This is what I am now! There’s no going back from it, so take a long fucking look !” “Oh my God,” Diane cried. “This isn’t you. This isn’t you! My Kevin would never —” “No, I’m not your Kevin. He died on the operating table.” He turned and stalked away from her . The door burst open, and several technicians hurried into the room. Diane, in tears, was escorted out . Kevin glanced over his shoulder, the plates of his jaw shifting as though he were clenching his teeth. He closed his eyes. The video went black . Several seconds passed, and an image flickered on. A dark-haired woman with brown, almond-shaped eyes leaned back in her seat, pinching the bridge of her nose. She bore a faint resemblance to Nancy Cooper. The bookshelves behind her marked the room as some sort of office . “My name is Doctor Jessica Yuan. I’m a neurologist, and I led the team that pioneered the method by which Kevin Randall Turner, aged forty-three, had his consciousness transferred into…a robotic surrogate body. The procedure, at its core, was successful. There are thousands of hours of video documenting both the procedure and our work with Mr. Turner during his recovery .” She leaned forward, resting her elbows atop the desk, and raked her fingers through her hair. “I’m making this recording because the future of this project is uncertain. The war has spilled onto American soil, and the military has shown increasing interest in our work — particularly that of my colleague, Doctor William Anderson, who has made great advances in robotics and artificial intelligence. It’s inevitable that they’re going to seize our research, and there’s not a damned thing we can do about it .” Sighing, she ran her gaze over the bare desktop . “We didn’t foresee the consequences. We pushed so hard for this, and…we should’ve known. I should’ve known. Mr. Turner’s mental state is extremely unstable and volatile. He often refuses to work with us, demanding to see his wife and children, but his psychiatrists — Doctors Foster and Kuering— are convinced that he’s a danger to his family. He came close to harming his wife on her last visit, six weeks ago. That’s when he tore off his face .” Swallowing thickly, she eased back into the chair and placed her hands atop the desk . “It’s apparent that the magnitude of the changes he endured were too much for his mind to cope with. Everything is different for him. He sees, hears, and feels in ways that no human was meant to. The everyday bodily functions of his old life are gone, and it’s given his already fragile mind excessive time to wander. He rages against what he is , and clings to what he was .” “Perhaps it’s a matter of…mental toughness? We don’t know. He’s the only successful candidate. Maybe it would break anyone to go through this... All I know is that he is dangerous. God, I can’t imagine what would happen if the government tried to use this to produce soldiers .” Her head sagged, strands of dark hair falling into her face. “This isn’t what I wanted. You have to believe, it’s not what any of us wanted. This was supposed to help people. He told us in his final pre-op interview that the risks were worth the chance at more time with his family. And now…he is, effectively, immortal, and he cannot spend any of that time with the people he loved the most . “He’d said that, if he had died during the procedure, it would have spared them his suffering. And what have I done, but tainted their last memories of him ?” She tilted her head back. Her eyes sparkled with tears. “I’m sorry,” she rasped, and reached for the camera. The feed cut out . Heavy silence fell over the war room. Ronin’s processors poured over the information. What kind of world had this been, before ? “We thought it important for all of you to see this,” Newton said, “because we want you to understand that he was, once, a man. I worked closely with Doctor Anderson throughout the procedure, and I am the one who reactivated Kevin Turner, with full knowledge of his history. I hoped he would find a new purpose in the rebuilding of this world. I was wrong in my assessment of him . “I cannot say with any certainty whether he recalls his previous life. He made no indication of such during our brief interactions after I made him operational. Regardless, it is clear that he has violently distanced himself from humanity. Please, make no mistake — he operates with malice, not the cold, logical front he projects .” Newton tilted his head down, optics on the table. “I normally would not condone violence. Doctor Anderson did not program it into my functions when he created me, and our work together was performed in the hopes of achieving a golden age of prosperity and comfort. There is a chance that, somewhere deep within his coding, some of Kevin Turner remains in Warlord. There is a chance he can be reasoned with .” Lara stiffened, fingers digging into Ronin’s hand . “But I cannot ask any of you to risk your safety on that chance. Kevin Turner suffered unduly, and all our good intentions cannot reverse that. It is time for his suffering to end .” Beside Ronin, Lara released a long, slow, unsteady breath, and the tension in her grip eased . It was natural for bots to calculate odds, to weigh probability heavily in their decisions. Perhaps Ronin’s risk-assessment functions had been damaged in the Black Out — he actively sought danger by going into the Dust, and should have been ended a thousand times over. But even he could recognize the odds . The attackers were far more likely to die to a man than Warlord was to be swayed by words . “I’m sure most of you have questions,” Rodriguez said, drawing all eyes back to him. “I do, too, but we don’t have the time or the information to answer all of them. Still, before I dismiss you, I want to reiterate this…there are people suffering in that town, every day. People who don’t know when their next meal might be. People who don’t have someone like Nancy to take care of them when they’re sick. People who wake up every day to the likelihood that it will be their last . “We pull this off, and we’re helping all those people. But we’ll be securing our own future, as well. This place was never meant to be used as a permanent settlement. We need the sun. We need fields for our crops. We need open air. And we need to reach out, to broaden our community, if we want it to flourish .” The room quieted again. Ronin ran his optics over the soldiers. Many of them were young, and had likely never exchanged fire with hostile forces. How many of them would be dead before this was over? How many were realizing their mortality, even now ? “When do we leave?” Lara asked . “Three hours before dawn. Get your gear prepped tonight, and get to sleep early.” Rodriguez stood. “ Dismissed .” Still holding Lara’s hand, Ronin led her out of the room amidst the small gathering of uniformed soldiers. As they wound through the corridors, the crowd thinned, until he and Lara were alone in a long, silent hallway . Data, new and old, whirred through his processors, almost too fast for him to keep up with. It was sorted, sifted, classified, and redirected into countless simulations, compared to countless potential outcomes . Tomorrow, they would march into Cheyenne, the stronghold of a monster who’d killed dozens, if not hundreds, of innocent humans and bots during his reign. The monster who’d murdered Tabitha. Who would have ended Ronin and Lara, were it not for Newton and sheer chance . Ronin had already acknowledged the near-impossibility of Lara’s very existence, the inconceivable chains of cause-and-effect stretching through epochs to result, ultimately, in the woman he loved. Their survival was a similar collection of chance occurrences . The sudden storm, their proximity to the farmhouse, Lara’s discovery of the shelter behind it. Newton’s presence within. His decision to remain hidden while the gearheads attacked, and their decision not to search the shelter. Newton’s guidance in locating the base and Captain Cooper’s willingness to assist strangers in need. Nancy’s dedication and talent . Any one of those things might have been offset by the slightest change at any point in time . His CPU linked it, suddenly, to a wholly human concept, one that had always been outside his comprehension . Fate . As they stepped into their room, Lara withdrew her hand from his and turned to him. “What’s running through that head of yours ?” “Nothing,” he replied. “My central processing unit and data storage are located in my torso .” She smirked. “It’s a saying. You always take everything so literally.” She nudged him before sitting on the bed . “Perhaps you need to familiarize yourself with my anatomy a little better.” Ronin smirked , too . Laughing, she leaned back on her elbows and wiggled her brows. “Maybe I should .” Despite her playful tone, her smile was strained, and a shadow lurked in her eyes. She always projected strength and stubbornness, never backed down from a fight. But that did not mean she was without fear . He eased down beside her. “Honestly, there’s not enough time in this day, month, or year to tell you what’s going through my mind. You insist on going tomorrow, and I know nothing I say will stop you. We’ll be over a mile apart, amongst enemies. I have to accept that I cannot be there to protect you, and must somehow entrust that task to others .” “It’s the right thing to do, Ronin. Most of those people know me. They’re more likely to trust me, knowing I’ve lived in the same shit hole, over some strangers with guns .” “I know it’s the right thing,” he said. He shifted onto his knees and leaned over her, slipping his fingers into her hair. He stared into her eyes, into their intricate, delicate weave of vibrant blues and subtle grays. “But I don’t want to do it .” Those eyes flickered over his features as though searching for the answer upon his face, and a small crease appeared between her brows. She shifted, moving her arms out from beneath her, and pressed her palm to his cheek . “ Why ?” “Because you are too great a price to pay for their freedom .” She looked away, frowning, and the crease between her brows deepened. Her lower lip quivered . “I don’t want to die,” she said thickly. “After everything we’ve been through, after finding you…I want to live . More than ever.” Tears had gathered in her eyes when she looked at him again. “But my life is a flicker compared to yours. You’ll keep going on, and I’ll be dead in fifty years, or thirty, or ten. Or tomorrow. And so will all those people. They deserve a chance , too .” Ronin smoothed back the hair from her face, wiping her tears with his thumb as they fell. When he first met her, he didn’t understand such things. He was a bot without a true purpose. Despite his logic-defying treks into the wastes, he could never have comprehended her reasons for wanting to return to Cheyenne . “Your life is my purpose,” he said, “and I think I understand why you’re willing to risk it. I disagree with you putting yourself in danger, but because it’s important to you, I will go, and I will do whatever is necessary.” Ronin leaned his forehead against hers. “So long as I function, you will not die tomorrow, nor any other day, for many, many years .” Lara exhaled shakily and embraced him. “I’ll try to keep out of trouble .” Ronin smiled. “Don’t lie to me, Lara Brooks. You’re going there specifically to cause trouble .” “Okay, out of unnecessary trouble , then .” She slipped her hand beneath his shirt, fingertips grazing his abdomen. The sensation danced across his sensors. Her eyes and cheeks were reddened from her tears, but he’d never seen her so beautiful as she was in that moment . “What will we do after all this?” she asked . It was not something he’d considered yet; his processing power was so caught up in the countless scenarios for the next day that he hadn’t devoted any thought to the day after. Their success — and their survival — was too uncertain . “I don’t know,” he said, after a long pause . “I want to return to your house .” Ronin tilted his head. “After all this, you still want to go back to Cheyenne ?” She flattened her palm against his stomach. “It was never the place, it was the ruler. Without Warlord, Cheyenne can be rebuilt. Your house…it became home for me. A real home, and it’s where I fell in love with you .” The frantic simulations slowed, his processors shifting toward the present. Toward Lara . Death wasn’t an issue for bots. As he’d once told her, there was on and off . Despite his core programming’s tendency — oft ignored — to err on the side of self-preservation, he did not fear his eventual deactivation. Hell, he’d been through it at least twice, already . But the idea of her death, of a future without Lara…that instilled true fear inside him. Irrational fear, the kind that whispered in distant corners of his consciousness, preying upon his inadequacies. How could he revert to a directionless life after she was gone ? “Then that is where we’ll stay,” he replied. “Though…if you still want to go, I promised I would take you to the ocean .” “Yes. I want to see the endless ocean, like in that book.” Her hand slid higher, sweeping over his chest and brushing his nipples. She shifted her hips, skirt falling to reveal her thighs, and smoothed her foot along his calf. “We can see the ocean, and then go back home. We could even tell the neighbors about it, but most of them probably wouldn’t believe us .” Electric jolts of pleasure pulsed through Ronin as she caressed his skin. Holding himself up on one arm, he placed a hand on her thigh and skimmed it toward her hip, bunching the fabric of her skirt around his wrist. She shivered beneath his touch, releasing a soft, shuddering breath . “It won’t matter what they think,” he said, focusing his optics on her lips . “Not even a little.” She spread her knees, opening herself to him . “You’re all that matters, Lara Brooks .” She smiled. Her hand stopped at the center of his chest, directly over his core processors. “Love me, Ronin .” He eased over her, nestling his hips between her thighs . “Long after the Dust claims me .”
